title: leptochiton quitoensis
intro: Leptochiton quitoensis, also known as the Quito leathery fern, is a type of plant species that belongs to the family of ferns and is native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Its health benefits include the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Leptochiton quitoensis can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in folk remedies for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
